loopbacks.reason: Implementer-reported frozen-test defect (ADR-009 route, shift-left before review): TEST-755 in tests/agent-structure.test.ts triggers its confinement check on the raw substring src.includes('node-pty'), which contradicts REQ-011's own surface - args.ts MUST contain the literal 'node-pty' to parse the --pty=node-pty flag value (TEST-759 requires it), and main.ts must import './node-pty-backend' by name to select the backend. The REQ-011 intent is confinement of the node-pty MODULE reference (import/require/dynamic-import specifier exactly 'node-pty'), not of the substring. Fix: key the trigger on extracted import specifiers === 'node-pty' (the existing importSpecifiers helper), keeping the static-import/require prohibitions and the lazy dynamic-import pin unchanged, plus an anti-vacuity existence assertion for node-pty-backend.ts. No production code change expected from this loopback; the implementation (closure-handle fake backend, session, entry) is NOT condemned.

implementer.reason: Router requested A/B (codex:codex-rescue + orky:implementer) on the networking+determinism concerns, but this driver environment has no parallel agent-dispatch tool and one shared worktree (two concurrent implementers would collide); codex exec additionally has a recorded stall precedent on this box (0016 implement re-entry 3: backgrounded codex sat on stdin and wrote nothing, killed at the 600s bound) and per ADR-025 codex never holds the pipeline. orky:implementer (Claude, driver-inline) ran solo: 10 source/config/doc artifacts, one ADR-009 tests loopback en route (TEST-755 substring-trigger defect, fixed at the tests phase), implement gate re-derived green (build+test+both freezes) on the final tree; codex is reserved for a bounded read-only verifier cross-check at review.
